svg.text: THE ORDER BOOK — depth ladder (one per symbol: AAPL)
best bid 150.50 ≥ best ask 150.50 → they CROSS → matching fires (trade @ 150.50)
spread = bestAsk − bestBid; normally > 0 (rest on book); here 0 ⇒ trade
within ONE price level → FIFO by arrival time (that is the "time" in price-time)
O(log n) offer / poll · O(1) peek best · one book isolates one symbol

g.text: filled? → poll from heap, status = FILLED
} loops until the book no longer crosses ✓

svg.text: filled qty accrues · remainder rests on book
has a price · matches only if it crosses the book
unfilled remainder RESTS on book (price-time priority)
no price · takes BEST available now, sweeps levels
never rests — unfilled remainder is cancelled

g.text: → S1 remaining 0 ⇒ FILLED, poll off min-heap
→ B1 remaining 20 ⇒ PARTIALLY_FILLED, stays on book
synchronized methods on OrderBook (add / match / remove)
one lock PER book ⇒ different symbols match in parallel
update filledQty / record trade in the same critical section
race if unsynced: two takers poll the same resting order →
double-fill (sell 80 twice). The lock serialises the match loop.
scale-out: shard by symbol across engines (shared-nothing)
1) best PRICE wins (highest bid / lowest ask)
2) tie on price → earliest arrival wins (FIFO)
execute at the RESTING order's price, not the
incoming one — rewards the liquidity provider &
qty = min(remaining) each match. Leftover stays
on the book as PARTIALLY_FILLED until fully
matched or cancelled. One order → many trades.

svg.text: ① one OrderBook / symbol: bid max-heap + ask min-heap → ② match while bestBid ≥ bestAsk (FIFO within a level) → ③ trade @ maker price, min qty, partial fills → order states
Patterns: Priority Queue (heaps) · Callback (match) · Facade (StockExchange) · State (order lifecycle)
Stock Exchange — LLD · price-time priority matching engine
